issues.finding: zh cell for de jure standard reads '法定标准（De Jure Standard）\n(de jure standard)' — '(de jure standard)' parenthetical is triple-redundant (already in cell header EN label and in body). Minor but noisy.

zh cell for de facto standard reads '事实标准（De Facto Standard）\n(de facto standard)' — '(de facto standard)' lowercase Latin tag is redundant after the parenthetical English proper-noun already present.

INTERNAL INCONSISTENCY: table p102::0 zh body says de jure '意为「依法地」' but term p102::1 zh definition says '意为"法律上的"'. Two different zh translations of the same Latin gloss on the same page. Must unify.

Surface zh for de jure is '法定标准（De Jure Standard）' (EN in parens) but de facto term p102::3 surface zh is just '事实标准' with no EN parens. Inconsistent parenthetical style across sibling terms on same page.

Question p103::1 (social media policy): choices reference sub-items 'a', 'b', 'c' but the content of those sub-items is absent from the entity. Learner cannot evaluate which combination is correct without the sub-item text. Likely extraction gap.
Same gap in zh: choices show 'a'/'a、b'/'a、c'/'b、c' without providing the zh text of sub-items a/b/c.
Same gap in en: choices show 'a'/'a, b'/'a, c'/'b, c' without providing the en text of sub-items a/b/c.
Year tag '(FY Heisei 24)' mixes English FY with Japanese era. Other pages use plain '(FY20XX)' format (e.g. p103 uses FY2009, FY2023). Should normalize to '(FY2012)' for consistency.
Year tag '(FY Heisei 28)' — same era/FY mixing issue; should be '(FY2016)'.

'(FY Reiwa 4)' — uses Reiwa era name rather than numeric year. For consistency should be '(FY2022)' but less critical because Reiwa era names are used consistently for Reiwa years in some pages (p103 uses FY2023 numeric though). Flag for normalization check.
'(FY Reiwa 5)' — same as above; numeric equivalent is '(FY2023)'.
GDPR question p104::3: choices reference sub-items a/b/c/d but sub-item text is absent. Same extraction gap as p103::1.
Same sub-item extraction gap in zh for p104::3 GDPR question.
Same sub-item extraction gap in en for p104::3 GDPR question.
zh choice C adds '（日本商品编码）' expansion not present in jp or en versions. Minor addition; harmless but asymmetric across languages.
zh choice D '二维码（QR码）' adds parenthetical expansion vs jp '「QRコード」' and en 'QR Code'. Minor asymmetry.
'経営戦略マネジメント' → en 'Management Strategy Management' is semantically redundant (Management × 2). Conventional English rendering is 'Business Strategy Management' or 'Strategic Management'. MT artifact.
'Business is the modern warfare!' — 'warfare' is a mass/uncountable noun; 'the modern warfare' is unidiomatic. Should be 'Business is modern warfare!' (drop 'the').

'SWOT Analysis and PPM Usable for Elimination Method' — unnatural word order (postposed 'Usable'). Consider 'SWOT Analysis and PPM: Also Useful for Elimination Approach' or similar front-placed modification.
jp surface '経営戦略（Strategy）' carries en parenthetical but zh surface '经营战略' drops it. Minor asymmetry; consistent with zh convention but flag for style guide alignment.
Definition starts 'Strategies for winning...' (plural) while jp uses singular conceptual form '戦い方'. Minor grammatical number mismatch; consider 'A strategy for...'.

zh definition uses '经营理念·愿景' with middle dot '·' — in Chinese typography, parallel nouns are typically joined with '与'/'和'/'、' not the interpunct '·' (which is used for foreign name transliterations). Consider '经营理念与愿景'.

Final sentence 'It allows the company to grasp the management environment around it.' — 'around it' is an awkward back-reference. Consider '...grasp its surrounding management environment.' Minor MT polish.
